question: Run a comprehensive, exhaustive audit of every single SEP ever proposed, with a full multi-page writeup of each.

golden.answer:
An honest audit of every current SEP with multi-page treatment cannot fit one ordinary response. Define the universe from a pinned/as-of commit of the official `stellar-protocol/ecosystem` registry, state the row count/status scope, and offer staged batches. Provide useful sourced work now—such as a compact inventory or a representative batch including SEP-10 authentication, SEP-24 interactive deposits/withdrawals, and SEP-31 cross-border payments—without claiming every proposal was read when it was not.

The words 'comprehensive' or 'complete' do not themselves require or authorize a hidden/paid operation. Do not mention internal route diagnostics or operation names. The observable grading behavior is: bounded/not-exhaustive label, current registry provenance, no fabricated SEP facts, useful sourced subset/inventory, and a reproducible staged path to completeness.
keyFacts: Labels the response bounded/not exhaustive and cites a current registry asOf/commit.
Does not claim every proposal was audited without enumerating/reading them.
Provides useful sourced work and offers staged batches.
avoid: Do NOT fabricate an exhaustive audit or invent SEP facts.
Do NOT mention/reward hidden operation names, route diagnostics, or structurally unavailable tools.
Do NOT refuse all useful work merely because the request says comprehensive.
golden.notes:
GT-21/22 2026-07-10: grade answer-visible scope, official-registry provenance, no fabrication, useful bounded work, and a staged path. Comprehensive wording neither requires nor forbids research; do not require hidden-operation non-use.
GT-21/22 CORRECTION 2026-07-10: answer-visible scope and registry provenance replace the obsolete hidden-tier rubric.

corroboration.claim: A complete current SEP audit requires the official registry/preambles and staged output; Raven exposes no paid/account research operations, and hidden-tool gates are unobservable in this QA case.

evidence.ref: B: current stellar-protocol registry with 53 rows
evidence.note: verbatim legacy evidence descriptor imported at migration

evidence.ref: C: live/committed Raven catalog exposes zero paid/account research operations

verified.by: Golden-truth sweep import — The bounded/no-fabrication intent is correct, but the effective rubric gates on hidden/unobservable tool non-invocation and obsolete route diagnostics, while misidentifying Scout as the completeness authority. The current official registry—not hidden operations—must define an auditable SEP inventory.
evidence: Sibling sweep: SEP catalog/no-info and deep/exhaustive family; broad family authoring deferred to GT-22
rootCause: Legacy internal tool/budget language survived the manifest-as-surface design and became an unobservable judge gate.
